paragraphs.text: Dear Hello, I have a few things I want to comment on. I have sorted them according to how important they seem to me. First, the protection of children from gambling, and I mean freely accessible games that have elements of hazard. Here I think the train travelled metaphorically. On the online rulet, you need to prove that he was 18, but just download a mobile game and probably get a bike of happiness, which you can turn to once a day for free and a second time for money. The problem is that those who profit from this always find a suite, even if no one makes it easier for them to match loot boxes with kinderects. The difference between casinos and these games is that the casino sends your money back from time to time. I feel that anything less than a licence equal to that of microtanning casinos will not solve this issue and always find a path. Perhaps as a father, I am very radical, but here they are children. The second thing concerns me as the software is sold, in particular games, what they are supposed to destroy themselves. Some games will tell you directly in licensing terms that the game will cease to work at any time when the publisher is trapped. Ending the aid may mean that everything works for you before, but it can mean that everything breaks down. As a student, I bought two desktop computers, and laptops both had windows 7 to close the support windows 7. The laptop could be disconnected from the internet, but Steam was installed on the desktop. Since then, STEAM has been the only launch programme requiring internet access. Yes Steam also has offline fashion, but it requires updates from time to time. However, the existence of offline fashion makes it even more insultable, as steam could easily leave everything in offline mode and not destroy the operating system. The time that Steam announced in advance was sufficient to prepare but had to do just as follows? The cessation of the aid in this case meant that the games that I had triggered by steam did not work, which I was aware of the notification, but unfortunately, for me, the need to stop installing the operating system. It seems to me that it was not intentional on the part of Steam to have had some changes in the application that they knew that they did not understand with windows 7 and therefore they did not bring it into updates earlier than half a year before the end of the programme support (functionality). A different terminology will need to be defined here. I propose: end of support – the programme will work without updates and something other than we may later cause the end of functionality – the programme ceases to function by this date. In the case of cars, the closing of the service does not affect whether the car starts. At least I hope that this is still the case. I heard that John Deere tried to change the tractor. The reasons given for that action were based on the fact that it was dangerous to have a computer with windows 7 connected internet. But why Steam requires to be connected was the only program for which the computer was connected to the internet and the developers could easily repair it. It was enough to set off the off-line steam mode not to require you to subscribe, but instead decided to inform you that at the end of the year the programme would not be operational. Who wants to look for ways and who does not want to look for reasons. They will claim that it is difficult or impossible to set up not to require you to subscribe. So that our programme does not happen, let us break it up for you to be sure at the end of the year. It’s what I saw from Steam. Most publishers will test a similar logic. That I cannot call Steam a virtual shop only in recent changes to licensing conditions. The cessation of aid is something Steam can do, I do not want to prevent it from doing so, but it is the facka that is particularly easy to stop the aid. I try to explain this on the theoretical electricity shop, which allows a purchased appliance to be picked up for an unlimited period of time. You buy the appliance online and do not export it. The trade writes that it closes in three months. If you don't collect it. — end of support will also come for appliances at home – discontinuing functionality
